content.problemStatement:
Mamalesh likes to drink mango lassi when it's hot, and only when it's hot.
If (and only if) the temperature on a given day is **strictly greater** than 353535 degrees, Mamalesh will drink mango lassi.

Mamalesh sees that today's temperature is XXX degrees Celsius. Will he drink mango lassi today?

Print `"Yes"` if he will, and `"No"` otherwise (without quotes).


Input Format

The only line of input will contain a single integer XXX, denoting today's temperature.


Output Format
Print `"YES"` if Mamalesh will drink mango lassi today, and `"NO"` otherwise (without quotes).


Each letter of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, the strings `NO`, `no`, `No`, and `nO` will all be treated as equivalent.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X (1 ≤ X ≤ 50), the temperature for that day.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing "YES" if X > 35, otherwise "NO". Letter case does not matter.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There are two test cases. For X=35 the temperature is not greater than 35, so output NO. For X=38 it is greater, so output YES.
Three test cases: 36>35 → YES, 35 is not greater → NO, 50>35 → YES.
Single test case with the minimum temperature 1, which is not greater than 35.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each temperature X, compare it with the threshold 35. If X is strictly greater, output YES; otherwise output NO. This is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time comparison per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
